questions.question: Explain the DDA (Digital Differential Analyzer) line drawing algorithm with an example.
keyPoints: DDA computes differences in x and y coordinates (Δx, Δy)
Calculates number of steps as max(|Δx|, |Δy|)
Computes increments: xinc = Δx/steps, yinc = Δy/steps
Iteratively plots pixels by adding increments and rounding

questions.question: State and explain the initial decision parameter formula in Bresenham's line algorithm.
keyPoints: P₀ = 2Δy - Δx where Δx = x₂-x₁ and Δy = y₂-y₁
If P₀ < 0: choose pixel at (xk+1, yk) - East
If P₀ ≥ 0: choose pixel at (xk+1, yk+1) - North-East
Decision parameter updated at each step

questions.question: Why is Bresenham's algorithm preferred over DDA for line drawing?
keyPoints: Bresenham uses only integer arithmetic (addition, subtraction, bit shifting)
DDA uses floating-point arithmetic which is slower
Bresenham avoids rounding errors present in DDA
Produces same quality lines with better performance

questions.question: What is aliasing in computer graphics? How can it be reduced?
keyPoints: Aliasing is the stair-step effect (jaggies) on raster displays
Caused by approximating continuous lines with discrete pixels
Anti-aliasing reduces this by shading pixels partially near edges
Techniques include supersampling and using gray levels for edge pixels

questions.question: How does Bresenham's algorithm handle lines with slope greater than 1?
keyPoints: For slope > 1, y becomes the major axis (increments by 1 each step)
Swap roles of x and y in the algorithm
Compute x incrementally instead of y
This maintains accuracy and avoids gaps in the line

questions.question: Explain the Midpoint line algorithm principle.
keyPoints: Uses implicit equation of line: ax + by + c = 0
Checks if midpoint between two candidate pixels lies above or below the line
If midpoint is below line: choose upper pixel; if above: choose lower pixel
Uses integer arithmetic similar to Bresenham

questions.question: Draw a line from (2,3) to (8,6) using DDA algorithm and show the pixel coordinates.
keyPoints: Δx = 6, Δy = 3, steps = max(6,3) = 6
